abstract: Social media platforms are facing increasing numbers of cyber-adversaries seeking to manipulate online discourse by using social bots to help automate and scale their attacks. Likewise, some social media users have developed capabilities to identify social bot activity at varying degrees of confidence. We exploit this user intelligence to augment traditional bot detection systems. Furthermore, not all crowd-generated labels are of equal value or credibility. Some individuals are quite adept at identifying social bot activity, whereas others may become merely suspicious but remain uncertain. We design a system inspired by speech act theory to evaluate which crowd-generated labels are most credible for augmenting bot detection system efficacy.

affiliation: Department of Information Systems, Arizona State University, Tempe, Arizona 85287
authors_full.name: Victor Benjamin

affiliation: Department of Information Systems, Arizona State University, Tempe, Arizona 85287
authors_full.name: T. S. Raghu

ob.ai_reason: 社交机器人检测系统，AI信息过滤技术应用
ob.tldr: 利用社交媒体用户对机器人活动的识别能力，提出基于言语行为理论评估众包标签可信度的系统，以提升自动机器人检测效果。实验证明，选择高可信标签可显著增强检测准确性。

title: Augmenting Social Bot Detection with Crowd-Generated Labels
title_zh: 基于群体生成标签的社交机器人检测增强方法
